violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. Date of last shellfish served were lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. Inspector provided applications. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025
The handwashing sinks at the bar are lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Inspector observed date of last shellfish used was missing from tags. PIC stated that staff discarded older tags. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Raw animal foods (raw, breaded chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cooked pasta) in the cold drawer refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Staff relocated items during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine, can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Shellfish and meats are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Working containers of dry and liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Staff labeled containers during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back stairway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
Ice condensate was observed in direct contact with packaged food in the walk-in freezer. Any foods where packaging has been compromised to be discarded. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -prep unit (work order in place per PIC) -gaskets to several refrigeration units -Reach-in refrigerator held with tape (at center between doors). New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -gaskets of several refrigeration units in the kitchen -bottom shelves of refrigerators -under prep unit lids. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
A hole was observed in the foundation wall in the downstairs hallway. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025
